candidates.why: Operating D&A summary row exists, but there is no selected COGS reference bucket and nothing in the label indicates it is COGS/cost-of-sales specific; adding it to COGS would risk misclassification.
Separate operating depreciation row, but it appears to be a broad operating line rather than a COGS-specific D&A line; no evidence it sits alongside a selected COGS bucket.
Could be operating-related, but the excerpt does not show it as COGS-specific and values are blank in the preview; not appropriate to add to COGS on this evidence.

variables.notes: No COGS row was selected earlier, and no visible D&A row is explicitly tied to cost of sales/cost of revenue. Conservative choice is to add nothing separately to COGS.

candidates.why: This is the clearest operating D&A summary row at the same level as other operating expense lines. Earlier XSGA selection explicitly excluded depreciation/amortization, so this separate summary row should be added later to the SG&A bucket. Preferred over component rows to avoid double counting.
Component of the broader D&A amount. Not chosen because the statement also shows the summary row 'Depreciation/Amortization', which should be preferred.
Granular amortization component that appears to roll into the broader D&A total; should not be added separately when summary row exists.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Licenses, Copyrights, Property Rights & Designs
candidates.why: Granular amortization component that appears to belong within total operating D&A; summary row is preferable.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket consists of 'Sales & Admin.' and 'Personnel Expenses', and prior mapping notes explicitly excluded depreciation/amortization. The income statement shows a separate operating D&A summary row above operating profit, so it should be added separately to XSGA. The summary row is chosen instead of 'Depreciation' plus amortization components to avoid double counting.
